summary: The actual screenshot partially matches the wireframe in terms of overall style and basic elements like title and buttons, but shows significant deviations including missing required sections for maximum commanders and lobby options. The wireframe itself is poorly suited for validation as it primarily displays textual descriptions rather than visual UI controls.

correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe feature a dark starfield background consistent with the global style requirements.
Thin green panel borders are present in both, matching the semi-transparent black panels and terminal-style aesthetic.
The title 'Create Lobby' appears in both, centered at the top of the main panel.

incorrectElements.expected: A dedicated section with label, description, and input control for map selection.
incorrectElements.actual: Present in actual with label, description, and 'Default' input, but wireframe only shows textual description without visual control representation.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe does not depict the map selection as a simple rectangle control, hindering precise layout comparison.

incorrectElements.expected: Section for selecting maximum commanders as per screen description.
incorrectElements.actual: Missing entirely in both actual screenshot and wireframe.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Required element from screen description is absent, preventing validation of this functional aspect.

incorrectElements.expected: Section for lobby options as per screen description.
incorrectElements.actual: Missing entirely in both actual screenshot and wireframe.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Required element from screen description is absent, indicating incomplete implementation and wireframe.

incorrectElements.expected: Create and Cancel buttons with labels matching description.
incorrectElements.actual: Actual has 'CREATE LOBBY' and 'CANCEL'; wireframe has 'CREATE / CONTINUE' and 'BACK / CANCEL'.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Button labels differ, and wireframe uses slashes indicating alternatives, which does not match the precise requirements.

incorrectElements.expected: No application-level title outside the screen title.
incorrectElements.actual: Wireframe includes 'RiskyStars Fleet command interface' at the top, absent in actual.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Extra element in wireframe not specified in requirements, causing mismatch in layout and content.

incorrectElements.expected: Fixed screen-relative geometry at 1536x832 with precise panel placement.
incorrectElements.actual: Actual panel is narrower and positioned differently; wireframe has a large text block in the center instead of controls.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Differences in bounds and nesting prevent accurate validation of spatial UI requirements.

incorrectElements.actual: Actual has subtitle 'Define the session shell before inviting commanders'; wireframe has long descriptive paragraph.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Content and presentation differ significantly, with wireframe not using terminal-style labels properly.

incorrectElements.expected: Wireframe provides precise baseline for all elements.
incorrectElements.actual: Wireframe is text-heavy and lacks control representations.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Even if actual matched closely, the abstract wireframe limits ability to validate DPI, scaling, and z-order precisely.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe primarily consists of textual descriptions rather than visual representations of UI controls like rectangles for inputs and selections, making it inadequate for precise layout and element validation against the screen description and global style requirements.
missingRequirements: Visual depiction of maximum commanders selection
Precise control rectangles for map selection
recommendedWireframeChanges: Regenerate wireframe to include simple rectangles representing all specified controls (map selection, max commanders, lobby options).
Remove extraneous titles like 'RiskyStars Fleet command interface' unless specified.
Ensure button labels match the exact requirements without alternatives.
Use terminal-style labels and semi-transparent panels consistently for all elements.
